exits.label: ◂ back down to the last torch

npcs.name: Master Cobb
npcs.title: who has the vision and cannot build it
npcs.hail: *(a soul that was plainly a builder in life crouches over a half-raised cairn with the fevered focus of an artist who can see the finished work perfectly and cannot, for the life or death of it, make the stones obey — and each grand block it sets atop the pile slides off and tumbles, again and again, and it begins again, again and again, building the same doomed monument to a vision it cannot make hold)*

npcs.name: Goodwife Pell
npcs.title: who hauls the stones and doubts
npcs.hail: *(a tired soul carries stone after stone to the architect's endless pile with the dogged patience of a laborer who long ago stopped believing the thing will ever stand and keeps hauling anyway, because the hauling is a kindness to a friend who cannot stop trying, and a soul has to spend its death on something, and tending another's hopeless hope is at least a warm way to waste it)*

npcs.name: a Newly-Dead
npcs.title: wandering, with no marker to find
npcs.hail: *(a soul drifts at the edge of the torchlight, freshly dead and utterly lost, turning in slow frightened circles the way the newly-dead do when there is no marker, no beacon, no raised stone to tell them which way leads to rest and which way leads down into the Hunger — and it is the living proof of why the cairn matters, and the unanswered question of why it will not stand)*

variants.text: *(It does not look up from the stone it is wrestling onto the pile — which promptly slides off and tumbles — and it begins again, talking half to you and half to the cairn.)* …*Hold.* *Hold*, *curse* you. *(the stone tumbles; it starts over)* …*Forgive* me. I *can't* — I *can't* *stop* in the *middle*, the *vision* *goes* if I *stop.* *(it sets another grand block; it slides)* I'm *Cobb.* *Master*-*builder*, in *life* — *raised* *temples*, *bridges*, a *lighthouse* the *Sword* *Coast* *still* *uses.* *(it wipes its brow, building)* And *here* — *here* I am *trying* to build the *most* *important* *thing* I *ever* *conceived* and it *WILL* *NOT* *STAND.* *(it gestures at the half-cairn, the tumbled stones)* A *beacon*, cold one. A *marker* — a *raised* *cairn* at this *crossing*, *tall* and *grand* and *unmissable*, so the *newly-dead* — *(it nods at the lost soul drifting at the torchlight's edge)* — so *THEY* don't *wander* *down* into the *Hunger* for *want* of a *sign.* *(its voice cracks with frustration)* I can *SEE* it, cold one. *Perfect.* *Finished.* The *grandest* *monument* the *dead* ever *raised.* And *every* *stone* I *set* *SLIDES* *OFF*, and I *don't* — I *don't* *UNDERSTAND* *why.* I *built* a *LIGHTHOUSE.* *Why* won't a *CAIRN* *STAND?*

nodes.speaker: Master Cobb
nodes.text: *(It pauses its endless building, just for you, hands grey with stone-dust that isn't quite real.)* …*Ask.* *(wearily)* But ask *quick* — the *vision* *fades* when I *stop*, and I have to *start* *over*, and I've *started* *over* more *times* than the *tide* has *turned.* *(it eyes the doomed pile)* *Maybe* you *see* something I *don't.* *Fresh* *eyes.* *Gods* *know* *mine* are *too* *full* of the *finished* *thing* to *see* the *broken* *one.*

choices.text: "Show me how you're building it, Cobb. What stones are you using — and why those?"

[INVESTIGATION] "A master builder whose cairn won't stand isn't failing at masonry — you raised a lighthouse, you know how stone stacks. So it's not the engineering. It's the *material.* These grand blocks you're using — where'd you quarry them? What are they actually *made* of? Because a marker of the dead isn't built of the same stuff as a lighthouse of the living."

*(the one who carries names, to the one whose stones won't hold)* "I think I know why it won't stand, Cobb — and it's not your craft, it's your *quarry.* You're building it of grand words: GLORY, REMEMBRANCE, the names of kings. Those are heavy and they look right and they *slide*, because a marker of the dead doesn't hold on grandeur. It holds on *true names* — the small real ones, the forgotten. I carry those. Build it of *those*, and I'll hand you the keystone myself."
"Why this, Cobb? You raised a lighthouse in life. Why spend your death on a cairn that won't stand?"
"Keep at it, Cobb. Let me look at the rest of your crew."

nodes.speaker: Master Cobb
nodes.text: *(It brightens, delighted to explain its craft, and hefts a grand block.)* …*How.* *(it sets the block; it slides; it doesn't notice, already reaching for the next)* I build it the way I built the *LIGHTHOUSE*, cold one — *grand* and *true* and *MEANT* to *last.* *(it gestures at the blocks)* *Each* *stone* is a — a *WORD.* A *great* *one.* *This* one — *(it lifts a heavy block)* — *GLORY.* The *glory* of the *honored* *dead.* *This* — *REMEMBRANCE.* *This* — the *NAMES* of the *fallen* *KINGS*, the *SAINTS*, the *MARTYRS*, *carved* *deep* and *proud.* *(fervent)* A *marker* must be *GRAND*, cold one, to *draw* the *eye* of a *frightened* *new-dead* *soul* *across* the *dark.* It must be *the* *most* *important* *thing* in *sight.* So I build it of the *most* *important* *things* there *ARE* — the *great* *words*, the *great* *names*, the *grandest* *stone* I can *quarry* from the *whole* *history* of the *honored* *dead.* *(it sets GLORY atop the pile; it tumbles; it blinks, baffled)* …And it *SLIDES.* *Every* *time.* The *grandest* *stones* *slide* the *FASTEST*, cold one — have you *noticed* that? The *bigger* the *word*, the *worse* it *holds.* *(it stares at the fallen GLORY)* …I *thought* it was *me.* My *failing* *craft.* But you *say* it *like* — *(it looks at you)* — *like* the *SLIDING* is *telling* me *something.* *Is* it? Is the *cairn* *REFUSING* the *grand* *stones?* *Why* would a *marker* *refuse* the *grandest* *words* there *are?*

nodes.speaker: Master Cobb
nodes.text: *(It freezes, the block half-lifted, and something dawns across its dust-grey face.)* …The *MATERIAL.* *(barely)* Not the *engineering.* The — *(it looks at the grand block in its hands as if seeing it new)* …I *quarried* these from the *HONORED* *dead*, cold one. The *kings*, the *saints*, the *great* *names* *everyone* *remembers.* *(slowly)* *GLORY.* *REMEMBRANCE.* The *biggest*, *grandest*, *most* — *(it sets the block down, staring)* — *most* *REMEMBERED* *stones* there *are.* *(its voice changes)* …And they *SLIDE.* *(it looks at the lost soul drifting at the torchlight's edge, then back at its pile)* …A *marker* is *FOR* the *new-dead.* The *frightened*, the *lost*, the *FORGOTTEN-to-be.* And I've been *building* it of *KINGS.* *(barely)* Of souls *so* *remembered* they need *no* *marker* at *ALL.* *(it presses dusty hands to its face)* A *lighthouse* of the *living* is built of *stone* — *dead* *stone*, *heavy*, *inert*, the *grander* the *better.* But a *marker* of the *DEAD* — *(it looks at you, the master-builder finally seeing his error)* — it's not *built* of *stone* at *all*, is it. It's built of — of *whatever* *holds* a *soul* in the *world.* And a *king's* *glory* *doesn't* *hold* a *soul* — the *king* is *fine*, the *king's* *remembered*, the *glory* just — *sits* there, *inert*, *grand* and *useless* and *SLIDING.* *(barely)* …What *holds*, cold one? If not *glory*, if not *grand* *names* — what's the *material* a *marker* of the *dead* is *truly* *built* *of?* I've a *vision* and a *quarry* and *forty* years of *craft* — and I've been *cutting* the *wrong* *STONE.*

nodes.speaker: Master Cobb
nodes.text: *(It frowns at your question, then shakes its head, already reaching for the next grand block.)* …The *material's* *FINE*, cold one. *Best* *stone* there *is* — *GLORY*, *REMEMBRANCE*, the *KINGS.* *(it hefts the block)* You *don't* *build* a *grand* *thing* of *small* *stone*, that's — that's *first-day* *apprentice* *folly.* *(it sets the block; it slides; it sighs)* *No*, the *material's* *sound.* It's the — it's the *BINDING*, *probably.* The *MORTAR.* I'll *find* the *right* *mortar*, *eventually*, and *then* the *grand* *stones* will *HOLD.* *(it starts over, undeterred)* *Mind*, *step* *back* — the *vision* *needs* *room* — I *have* to *get* it *RIGHT*, cold one, the *new-dead* are *WANDERING*, *look* at *that* *poor* *lost* *thing*, it *NEEDS* the *beacon* — *(it builds, and the grand stones slide, and it does not see why)* — *just* let me — let me *find* the *mortar.* The *stone's* *fine.* The *stone's* *ALWAYS* *fine.* It *MUST* be the *mortar.* *(it cannot yet hear that the stone itself is the error.)*

nodes.speaker: Master Cobb
nodes.text: *(It goes utterly still, and the grand block falls from its hands — and for the first time it does not reach for another. It looks at the tumbled GLORY, and the lost soul at the torchlight's edge, and then at you, and the whole baffled frustration of an age resolves into a single, devastating clarity.)* …*True* *names.* *(barely)* The *small* *real* *ones.* The *FORGOTTEN.* *(it sinks down beside the pile)* …*Of* *COURSE.* *Gods*, *of* *COURSE.* *(it laughs, wet and stunned)* I have been building a *marker* for the *FORGOTTEN* out of the *REMEMBERED*, and *wondering* why it *won't* *HOLD* — when the *whole* *point*, the *whole* *MATERIAL*, the *only* *stone* that *could* *EVER* *bind* a *beacon* for the *lost*, is the *names* the *lost* *are* *losing.* *(it grips your cold hand)* A *king's* *glory* *slides* because the *king* *needs* *no* *holding* — he's *held* by *ten* *thousand* *living* *mouths.* But a *forgotten* *soul's* *true* *name* — *small*, *plain*, *spoken* by *no* one — *THAT* *has* *weight*, cold one, *real* *weight*, the *weight* of a *thing* that will *vanish* if it *isn't* *held* — and a *stone* that *NEEDS* *holding* is the *only* *stone* that *BINDS.* *(fierce, radiant)* *Build* it of the *forgotten's* *true* *names*, and *each* *stone* *holds* the *next*, because *each* is a *soul* *clinging* *to* the *world*, and *clinging* is *what* *MORTAR* *IS.* *(it rises, transformed)* You *carry* them, you *said.* The *true* *names.* *(it gestures at the half-cairn)* Then *HAND* me the *keystone, cold* one — the *first* *true* *name* — and *watch* what a *master-builder* *does* when he's *finally* *given* the *RIGHT* *STONE.* *Forty* *years* of *craft* and I *never* *built* the *thing* that *MATTERED* — *because* I never *had*, until *now*, the *one* *material* a *beacon* for the *lost* is *MADE* of: the *names* *nobody* *else* *would* *carry.*

nodes.speaker: Master Cobb
nodes.text: *(It pauses its building, and the fevered focus gentles into something older.)* …*Why.* *(quietly)* Because of the *LIGHTHOUSE*, cold one. *(it almost smiles)* In *life*, I *raised* a *lighthouse* — *Sword* *Coast*, *forty* years it's *stood*, *ten* *thousand* *ships* *home* *safe* by its *light.* *Best* *thing* I *ever* *made.* *(its face falls)* And I *DIED* *proud* of it — *certain* I'd *spent* my *life* *well*, *guiding* the *living* *home.* And then I came *HERE*, cold one, and I *SAW* — *(it looks at the lost soul)* — I *saw* the *new-dead*, *wandering*, *lost*, *drifting* *down* into the *Hunger* for *want* of a *light*, and *NO* *ONE* — *no* one had *built* them a *lighthouse.* *(its voice cracks)* The *living* get *beacons*, cold one. *Harbors*, *lights*, *markers* on *every* *dangerous* *shore.* And the *DEAD* — the *dead* get *NOTHING*, *just* *dark* *water* and a *Hunger* and *no* *light* to *steer* by. *(fierce)* So I *thought*: I *built* the *living* their *beacon.* I'll *build* the *DEAD* *theirs.* *One* *last* *lighthouse*, for the *souls* who *need* it *MOST* and have *NEVER* *had* one. *(it looks at the doomed pile)* …And I *can't.* *Forty* *years* of *craft* and I *can't* make it *STAND.* *(barely)* It's the *only* *thing* I *ever* *wanted* to *build* that *MATTERED* *more* than the *lighthouse* — and it's the *one* *thing* my *craft* *can't* *raise.* *That's* *why* I *can't* *stop*, cold one. A *builder* who *dies* *unable* to *build* the *one* *thing* that *matters* *most* — *(it returns to its endless work)* — *what* is *that*, but a *soul* that *wasted* its *whole* *craft* *on* the *wrong* *lighthouse?*

nodes.speaker: Master Cobb
nodes.text: *(It nods, already turning back to the doomed pile, lifting another grand stone that will not hold.)* …*Look* *round.* *Aye.* *(it sets the stone; it slides; it begins again)* *Pell* *hauls* for me — *good* *soul*, *tired* *soul*, *stopped* *believing* it'll *stand* a *long* *while* *back* and *hauls* *anyway*, *bless* her. *(it nods at the laborer)* And the *lost* one — *(it nods at the drifting new-dead)* — *that's* *WHY*, cold one. *That's* the *whole* *why.* *Every* *day* the *cairn* *doesn't* *stand* is a *day* a *soul* like *THAT* *wanders* *down* into the *dark.* *(it builds, fevered)* So *go* on. *Look* *round.* But *come* *BACK*, cold one — *(a flicker of desperate hope)* — *come* *back* if you *figure* *out* the *thing* I *can't.* *Why* the *stones* *slide.* *Why* a *master-builder* *can't* *raise* the *one* *cairn* that *matters.* *(it sets a stone; it tumbles)* …*Forty* *years* of *craft.* And the *most* *important* *thing* I *ever* *tried* to *build* *won't* *hold* a *single* *stone.* There's a *LESSON* in that, cold one. I'm *too* *busy* *building* to *learn* it. *Maybe* *you've* the *distance* to *see* it *plain.*

conversations.title: Goodwife Pell, Who Hauls and Doubts

variants.text: *(It sets down the stone it's carrying and straightens its back with a tired sigh, glad of the excuse to rest.)* …You've been *talking* to *Cobb.* *(a wry, weary smile)* *Everyone* who *passes* *talks* to *Cobb.* He's the *one* with the *VISION.* I'm just the *one* who *hauls* the *stones* the *vision* *needs.* *(it nods at the doomed pile)* *Pell.* I *carry.* That's — that's the *whole* of my *part* in this. Cobb *sees* the *finished* *cairn*, *grand* and *tall*, and I *fetch* him *stone* after *stone* to *build* it, and the *stones* *slide* *off*, and he *starts* *over*, and I *fetch* *more.* *(it spreads tired hands)* I *stopped* *believing* it'd *stand* — *gods*, *ages* *back.* I'm *not* a *fool.* I can *see* it *never* *holds.* *(it looks at Cobb, fond and sad)* But he *CAN'T* *stop.* And a soul *has* to *spend* its *death* on *something.* So I *spend* mine *fetching* *stones* for a *friend's* *hopeless* *hope*, because — *(a tired shrug)* — because *letting* him *build* *alone* would be *crueler* than *fetching* for a *thing* that *won't* *stand.* *Company's* a *kindness*, even when the *work's* a *waste.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Pell
nodes.text: *(It sits on a hauled stone, grateful for the rest, and looks at you with tired, honest eyes.)* …*Ask.* *(a weary smile)* I've *time.* *Hauling's* *slow* *work* when you've *stopped* *believing* in the *building.*

choices.text: "You haul stones you don't think will hold. Why, really? Not 'company' — the true reason. Nobody spends a death on a thing they're sure is hopeless."
"You said you can see it never holds. You've watched every stone slide. So *you* might know what Cobb can't — what's different about the stones that slide worst? You've hauled them all."

*(the one who'd give the doubter a real stone to carry)* "What if it could stand, Pell? Not Cobb's hopeless hope — really stand. The reason it slides is the material: he's building it of grand words, and a marker for the lost holds only on true names. I carry those. If we build it right, the next stone you haul won't slide. It'll hold. Would you haul for *that* — not a friend's delusion, but a beacon that actually works?"
"Rest a while, Pell. The stones'll keep."

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Pell
nodes.text: *(It is quiet a moment, then gives you the truer answer.)* …The *true* *reason.* *(it looks at Cobb, building)* …*Cobb* was my *neighbor*, cold one. In *life.* *Forty* *years.* I *watched* him *raise* that *lighthouse* — *watched* him *pour* his *whole* *SELF* into it, *every* *stone* a *piece* of *him*, and when it *stood* — *(its voice softens)* — when it *STOOD*, and the *first* *ship* *came* *home* by its *light*, I *never* *saw* a *soul* so — so *COMPLETE.* *(quietly)* And then we *died*, and came *here*, and I *watched* that *same* *soul* *try* to *build* the *dead* their *lighthouse* — and *FAIL*, over and over, *forty* years of *craft* *sliding* off a *pile* that *won't* *hold* — and I *can't* — *(its voice catches)* — I *can't* *WATCH* him *do* it *ALONE*, cold one. A *soul* that *built* a *thing* that *guided* *ten* *thousand* *ships* *home*, *reduced* to *stacking* *stones* that *fall* — and *nobody* *helping*, *nobody* *fetching*, *nobody* *believing* *enough* to *carry* *even* *one* *stone* — *(barely)* — that's *too* *lonely* a *thing* to *let* a *friend* *suffer.* *(it meets your eyes)* So I *haul.* *Not* because I think it'll *stand.* Because *hauling* *next* to *him* means he's *not* *building* his *grief* *alone.* *(quietly)* *That's* the *true* *reason*, cold one. The *cairn's* *hopeless.* But the *company* in the *hopelessness* — *that's* *real.* It's the *one* *stone* in this *whole* *pile* that's *never* *slid.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Pell
nodes.text: *(It frowns, thinking — really thinking, a soul that has hauled every stone and never been asked what it noticed.)* …What's *different* about the *worst* *sliders.* *(slowly)* …*Huh.* *Nobody's* *asked* me *that.* Cobb *picks* the *stones*; I just *carry* what he *points* at. *(it considers)* But — now you *say* it — *(its tired eyes sharpen)* — the *GRANDER* ones *slide* *faster.* *Always.* The *biggest*, *heaviest*, *most* — the *GLORY* *stone*, the *KINGS' * *NAMES* — those *barely* *touch* the *pile* before they're *off.* *(slowly)* And the *little* ones — the *FEW* *times* Cobb's *grabbed* a *small* *plain* *stone* by *mistake*, in a *hurry* — *(its brow furrows)* — those *sat* a *moment* *LONGER.* *Held*, *almost*, before he *swapped* them for *something* *grander.* *(it looks at you)* …I *thought* it was *random*, cold one. The *sliding.* But it's *NOT*, is it. The *bigger* the *stone*, the *worse* it *holds.* *(it stands, the tired doubt sharpening into something else)* …That's — that's *BACKWARDS*, isn't it. In *life*, the *bigger* *stone* *holds* *BETTER* — *weight* is *what* *makes* a *wall* *stand.* But *here*, the *grand* *stones* *SLIDE* and the *small* ones *ALMOST* *HOLD.* *(it meets your eyes, the first hope in an age flickering)* …I don't *know* what it *means*, cold one. I just *haul.* But *YOU* — you've got that *look* — you *know* *something.* *The* *small* *stones* *hold*, *cold* one. *I've* *carried* *enough* of *both* to *SWEAR* it. *Whatever* *that* *tells* you — *USE* it. *I'm* *tired* of *hauling* *grand* *stones* that *fall.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Pell
nodes.text: *(It stares at you, and the tired resignation it has worn for an age cracks open into something it had stopped letting itself feel.)* …It *could* *STAND.* *(barely)* *Really* *stand.* *(it looks at Cobb, building his doomed pile, then back at you)* You're — you're *saying* it was *never* *hopeless.* It was just — *WRONG.* The *wrong* *STONE.* And the *RIGHT* *stone* — *true* *names*, the *forgotten* — would — *(its voice shakes)* — would *HOLD.* *(it presses a hand to its mouth)* *Gods*, cold one. Do you know what you've — I have *hauled* for *Cobb* for an *AGE*, *certain* it was *hopeless*, *certain* the *kindest* thing I could *do* was *keep* him *company* in a *DELUSION* — and you're telling me it was *never* a *delusion*, it was a *SOLVABLE* *PROBLEM*, and *all* this *time* if *someone* had just — *(it looks at the lost soul drifting at the torchlight's edge)* — if *someone* had *known* the *right* *material*, that *poor* *lost* *thing* would have a *BEACON*, and so would *every* *new-dead* *soul* that's *wandered* *down* into the *Hunger* while Cobb *stacked* the *WRONG* *STONES.* *(fierce, transformed)* *YES*, cold one. *YES*, I'll *haul* for *THAT.* Not a *friend's* *hopeless* *hope* — a *beacon* that *WORKS.* *(it seizes a stone, then drops it)* — *no* — not *THIS* *stone, the* *GRAND* *one* — *(it grins, fierce and wet)* — *tell* me the *right* *one*, cold one. The *true* *names.* *Hand* me the *FIRST* *one*, and I will *haul* *true* *names* to *Master* *Cobb* until this *crossing* has the *brightest* *beacon* the *DEAD* *ever* *raised* — and *not* *one* *more* *soul* *wanders* *down* for *want* of a *LIGHT.* *(it looks at Cobb, joyful)* …He's going to *WEEP*, cold one. *Forty* *years* of *sliding* *stone* — and the *cairn's* going to *STAND.* *Finally.* *Let's* *build* the *dead* their *lighthouse.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Pell
nodes.text: *(It nods, and picks up its stone again, but watches you go with less resignation than before.)* …*The* *stones'll* *keep.* *(a tired almost-smile)* *Aye.* They *keep.* They *FALL*, but they *keep.* *(it hefts the stone)* *Go* *on*, cold one. *Mind* the — *(it nods at Cobb)* — *mind* you *don't* *break* his *heart, if* you *can* *help* it. He's *fragile* under the *fever*, *Cobb.* A *soul* that *can't* *build* the *one* *thing* it *needs* to is a *soul* *one* *failure* from *giving* *up* *entirely.* *(quietly)* So if you've *NOTHING* *real* to *offer* — *don't* *offer.* *False* *hope* *to* *Cobb* would be *crueler* than the *honest* *sliding.* *(it meets your eyes)* But if you've *something* *TRUE* — *(a flicker)* — *gods*, cold one, if you've something *TRUE* — then *come* *back* *quick.* I have *hauled* *hopeless* *stone* for an *AGE.* I would *give* my *second* *death* to *haul* *ONE* that *HELD.*

variants.text: *(It turns its frightened circles toward you, reaching, lost.)* …*Please.* *(barely)* *Please* — which — which *WAY?* *(it gestures at the dark all around)* I *died.* I — I *think* I *died, there* was a — and *now* I'm *HERE*, and it's *DARK*, and there's *no* — there's no *ROAD*, no *sign*, no — *(it turns, panicked)* — *that* *way* there's a — a *PULL.* *Down.* *Cold.* It *WANTS* me, the *down-pull*, and I *don't* — I don't *KNOW* if *down* is *right* or *WRONG*, there's *nothing* to *TELL* me. *(it grips at you)* You're *cold* too — but *different* *cold*, *steady* *cold*, *not* the *down-pull* *cold* — are *YOU* the *sign? Are* *you* the *way?* *(barely)* I've been *turning* in *circles* since I — since I *got* *here.* *Looking* for a — a *LIGHT.* A *MARKER.* *Anything* that *says* *'this* *way, lost* *soul, this* *way* to *REST* and *NOT* *that* *way* to the *PULL.'* And there's — there's *NOTHING.* Just *dark*, and the *down-pull*, and *me*, *turning* and *turning* and *turning.*

nodes.speaker: a Newly-Dead
nodes.text: *(It clings to your presence, the one steady thing in its lost dark.)* …*help* me. *(barely)* I don't — I don't *know* how to *be* *dead.* *Nobody* — there was *nobody* *here* to *tell* me. *Just* the *dark* and the *pull.* *(it trembles)* *Ask* me — ask me *anything*, *just* — *just* *don't* *go.* The *turning's* *worse* *alone.*

choices.text: "Hold still, friend. The down-pull is the Hunger — that's the wrong way, and you knew it without being told. There's a crossing here, and souls trying to build you a beacon. You're not as lost as you feel. Stay near me a moment."
"What's your name, friend? Tell me — quickly, while you still have it. The Hunger takes the name first, and a name held is a soul that can't be pulled down."

*(the one who carries names, finding the keystone)* "Listen to me. The marker these souls can't build — it needs a first true name to hold, the keystone the whole beacon binds on. And you've just given me the most precious thing there is: a new-dead soul's true name, fresh, before the Hunger takes it. Let me carry yours to the cairn. You won't just be saved by the beacon, friend — you'll be the *first stone of it*, the name that makes it stand, so no soul after you ever turns in this dark alone."
"Don't drift toward the pull, friend. I'll be back — and there'll be a light by then. I promise."

nodes.speaker: a Newly-Dead
nodes.text: *(It stops turning. For the first time since it arrived, it goes still, anchored by your steadiness.)* …The *Hunger.* *(barely)* The *down-pull* is the — and I *knew.* I *KNEW* it was *wrong*, the *cold* of it, even with *nothing* to *tell* me. *(it draws a shaking breath it doesn't need)* …*A* *beacon.* They're *building* me a — *(it looks toward Cobb's doomed pile)* — *that's* — that's a *CAIRN.* Half-*built.* *(its voice wavers between hope and confusion)* But it — it *keeps* *FALLING.* I've *watched* it, *turning* my *circles* — the *grand* one *stacks* the *stones* and they *SLIDE*, and I — I *thought* even the *BEACON* was *broken*, even the *one* *thing* *meant* to *save* me *won't* *STAND.* *(it grips you)* But you're — you're *steady, cold* one. You're *not* *sliding.* *(barely)* *Stay* *steady.* *Please.* If *you* don't *slide* — if *one* *thing* in this *dark* will *just* *HOLD* *still* — then *maybe* I can — maybe I can *stop* *turning* *long* enough to — to *learn* how to be *dead* *without* *drifting* *down.* *(it presses close to your cold)* You're the *first* *thing* that *held.* *Be* the *marker*, *cold* one, *till* there's a *real* one. *Just* — *don't* *slide.* I've *had* *enough* of *things* that *slide.*

nodes.speaker: a Newly-Dead
nodes.text: *(It clutches at the question like a rope.)* …My *NAME.* *(barely)* I — *(panic flares)* — it's — it's *GOING*, isn't it. I can *feel* it — the *down-pull*, it's — it's *pulling* the *NAME* *first*, I can *feel* it *thinning* — *(it grips you, desperate)* — *quick* — *quick*, before — it's — *(it reaches, hard)* — *EDDIN.* *(it gasps)* *Eddin.* I'm — I'm *EDDIN.* *Fisher.* *Off* the — the *Reed-Walk* *boats.* *Drowned* — *(it shudders)* — *drowned* *yesterday*, the *net* *fouled* and I — *EDDIN.* *Hold* it, *cold* one. *PLEASE.* *(it presses the name at you like a drowning thing pressing a child up out of the water)* I can *feel* it *going* — the *Hunger's* *got* a *grip* on it — but if *YOU* *hold* it, if *you* *carry* *'Eddin'* *somewhere* the *pull* *can't* *reach* — then I'm — then I'm *still* *SOMEBODY*, even *turning* in the *dark.* *(barely)* *Eddin.* *Eddin* the *fisher.* *Don't* *let* the *down-pull* *have* it. It's — it's *all* I *am* *now.* *A* *name* and a *direction* I'm *afraid* to *pick.*

nodes.speaker: a Newly-Dead
nodes.text: *(It stops turning entirely, and stares at you, and the panic gives way to something it did not expect to feel in the lost dark: purpose.)* …The *FIRST* *stone.* *(barely)* The — the *keystone.* The *name* that makes the *beacon* *STAND.* *(its voice fills with wonder)* You're — you're saying my *name* — *little* *Eddin* the *fisher*, *nobody*, *drowned* *yesterday* off the *Reed-Walk* — *MY* *name* could — could be the *thing* that *HOLDS* the *whole* *cairn* *up?* *(it laughs, shaky, transformed)* But I'm — I'm *NOTHING*, cold one. I'm a *fisher.* I *fouled* a *net* and *drowned.* There's *no* — no *glory* in me, no *grand* — *(it stops, understanding)* …*that's* *WHY.* *(barely)* That's *why* it *holds.* Because I'm *small.* Because I'm *forgotten-to-be.* Because my *name* *NEEDS* *holding*, and a *name* that *needs* *holding* is the *only* *stone* that *binds.* *(fierce, radiant)* *Carry* me, cold one. *Carry* *'Eddin'* to the *cairn* and *set* me at the *BOTTOM*, the *keystone*, the *first* *true* *name* — and *let* the *grand* *one*, *Cobb*, *build* the *rest* of the *forgotten* on *TOP* of me. *(it grips your cold hand)* I came *here* *lost*, *turning* in the *dark*, *certain* I was *nothing* and *nobody* would *ever* — and you're telling me I'm going to be the *FOUNDATION* of the *beacon* that *saves* *every* *soul* that *comes* *after.* *(weeping, glad)* I won't *drift* *down*, cold one. How *could* I? I'm the *KEYSTONE.* The *whole* *beacon* *stands* on *Eddin* *now.* *Set* me *down.* *Build* the *light.* And *tell* the *next* *lost* *soul* that *finds* its *way* by *us* — *(barely)* — tell them a *fisher* named *Eddin* is *holding* it *up.*

nodes.speaker: a Newly-Dead
nodes.text: *(It clutches at the air where your cold was, frightened to be left.)* …*Back.* *(barely)* You'll — you'll come *BACK.* *(it tries to believe it)* And there'll be a — a *light.* *(it looks at Cobb's falling stones, doubtful)* …*Will* there? *Truly?* *(it turns a slow, frightened circle)* *Don't* — *don't* be *long*, cold one. The *down-pull's* — it's *patient*, but it's *STRONG*, and I'm — I'm *so* *tired* of *turning*, and *resisting* the *pull* takes — it takes *everything* I've *got* *left*, and I've *not* *got* *much.* *(barely)* If you're *long* — if the *light* *doesn't* *come* — I *don't* *know* how *long* I can *keep* *NOT* *drifting* *down.* The *pull* is *easier* than the *turning.* That's the — that's the *terrible* *thing* about it, cold one. *Giving* *in* would be *RESTFUL.* *(it steadies, barely)* …*Hurry.* *Please.* *Build* the *light.* *Before* *resting* in the *Hunger* starts to *sound* like *mercy.* It's *starting* to *already.*
